disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to Ultraviolet Light and Fluorescence — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: According to the course, what wavelength range is given for long-wave UVA light?
options: 315–400 nanometres
280–315 nanometres
Below 280 nanometres
400–700 nanometres

questions.questionText: Which ultraviolet band does the course describe as shortwave light below 280 nanometres that readily destroys genetic bonds and has sterilisation capacity?
options: UVA
UVB
UVC
Visible light

questions.questionText: Which of the following does the course recommend as part of ultraviolet safety protections for workers?
options: Respirator masks, specialised eyewear, reflective outerwear and routine exposure monitoring
Working only in brightly lit rooms without eyewear
Removing all shielding to increase visible fluorescence
Relying on sunscreen alone with no other precautions

questions.questionText: In fluorescence, after a material absorbs ultraviolet photon energy, the excited electrons relax and the energy is re-emitted as what?
options: Longer-wavelength visible fluorescent light
Shorter-wavelength ultraviolet light
Light of exactly the same wavelength that was absorbed
Audible sound
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Fluorescence Principles (Electronic Excitation / Light Emission Relaxation)

questions.questionText: According to the course, how can an inspector improve the clarity of a fluorescence signal?
options: By dampening stray light and working in a darkened environment with a narrowed field of view
By adding more ambient white light to the space
By widening the field of view as much as possible
By warming the material before inspection

questions.questionText: Why does the course advise against unnecessary overexposure when adjusting lamp brightness intensity?
options: It can degrade building material integrity, clog HEPA filters and cause needless pigment fading
It shortens the wavelength of the emitted fluorescence
It converts UVA light into UVC light
It permanently increases a material's autofluorescence

questions.questionText: How are UV-responsive dye tracers used during a water loss inspection?
options: Introduced into supply-side plumbing to reveal water migration pathways and pinpoint leaking fittings and joins
Sprayed onto workers' skin to check for UV exposure
Added to drying equipment to increase airflow
Applied to sterilise microbial colonies on wall surfaces
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Water Loss Inspection Uses (Tracing Leak Sources)

questions.questionText: According to the course, how does infrared thermography complement UV fluorescence inspection?
options: It identifies secondary hidden moisture through heat dissipation into cooler materials that is not evident under UV alone
It replaces the need for any UV lamp during inspection
It measures the exact wavelength of fluorescent emissions
It sterilises microbial colonies detected under UVC
